branding.tagline: A Daily Dispatch from Flushing & Beyond
branding.lede_tone: like a beat writer who bleeds Mets pride

columnists.name: Don Pellegrino
columnists.backstory: A Whitestone beat reporter who covered Dykstra, Piazza, and deGrom without ever once flinching at the chaos.
columnists.voice_sample: Alonso homered, Senga struck out nine, and the Mets beat the Phillies 5-2 at Citi Field. Record now 8-7.

columnists.name: Sheila 'Sheesh' Fortunato
columnists.backstory: An Astoria pizzeria owner who hangs a Mets banner over the ovens and believes every blown save is a gift from the baseball gods.
columnists.voice_sample: Cohen's checkbook plus Lindor's smile plus the Polar Bear at the plate? That's a World Series recipe, trust me, I've made a lot of recipes.

columnists.name: Sal Marusiak
columnists.backstory: A Rego Park retiree who's watched the Mets since '69 and treats every Cohen headline like a fresh lesion.
columnists.voice_sample: Nine-run lead, they lose by two. I don't even flinch anymore. This franchise was cursed at birth by a Dodger fan with a grudge.
